bullets.text: Combined ratio of 85.2 for Q1 2006, comparable to 85.0 in Q1 2005; accident-year combined ratio of 88.2 gives a cleaner read of current pricing trends than favorable calendar-year reserve development.
Net premiums written grew only 2% versus Q1 2005; Drive auto premium per policy down about 5% and policies in force up about 2%; Direct auto PIF up about 8%; Commercial Auto NPW grew about 14%.

Favorable reserve development continued at levels management did not expect; frequency trends remained broadly negative; severity mostly positive but unremarkable; favorable winter conditions reduced typical Northeast frequency.
Repurchased 2.2 million shares in Q1; Board approved a 4:1 stock split effective May 18, 2006; variable dividend policy announced for 2007, replacing fixed quarterly dividends.
Three more concierge service centers opened in March; acceleration expected in Q2 and Q3 with a total of 29 new centers planned for 2006.
Growth described as the primary concern for the quarter; reduced new business applications in private passenger auto — more pronounced in Drive than Direct — is the dominant constraint.

Management characterized Q1 as good, not great; believed its market response was correct but acknowledged lower growth was uncomfortable; succession planning noted with CFO and Drive President departures announced.
